source_jobs.title: Join Our Team (Future Roles)
source_jobs.description:
Atlas is built for oncologists by oncologists. We provide practices with the staff, contracts, and the toolkit they need to deliver truly whole-person care to their cancer patients. Our value-based approach to care delivery aligns incentives - offering patients better outcomes, payors guaranteed reduction in medical expense, and provider partners greater financial independence.

As a fast-moving, early-stage company, we’re constantly evolving and adding new opportunities. If you don’t see a role that matches your background but are excited about our mission, we encourage you to apply here.

What we value in future teammates:

 - Adaptability and comfort with ambiguity in a fast-changing environment

 - A self-starter mindset and bias toward action

 - Ownership and accountability

 - Entrepreneurial spirit - resourceful, creative problem solvers

 - Passion for our mission and excitement to grow with the company

If this sounds like you, we’d love to connect. Apply now and let’s build the future of cancer care together.

source_jobs.title: Licensed Social Worker
source_jobs.description:
Role: Licensed Independent Clinical Social Worker (LICSW)

Company: Atlas Oncology Partners

Location: On-site; Rotating in-person clinic at Mobile and Baldwin County

Position Type: Full-Time



About Us

Atlas is built for oncologists by oncologists. We provide practices with the staff, contracts, and toolkit they need to deliver truly whole person care to their cancer patients. Our value-based approach to care delivery aligns incentives, offering patients better outcomes, payors guaranteed reduction in medical expense, and provider partners greater financial independence.



Role Description

At Atlas Oncology Partners, we believe compassionate, psychosocial care is just as essential as medical treatment in supporting patients with cancer. We are seeking a dedicated Licensed Independent Clinical Social Worker (LICSW) to provide counseling and emotional support to patients and their families throughout the cancer care continuum. This position plays a critical role in helping patients navigate the psychological, emotional, and social challenges associated with diagnosis, treatment, survivorship, and end-of-life planning. The LICSW partners with an interdisciplinary team to deliver evidence-based, patient-centered care rooted in empathy, collaboration, and clinical excellence. As a small nimble team, this role will also support care navigation duties to provide resource connection throughout the cancer treatment journey.



Responsibilities include, but not limited to, the following:

· Provide individual, family, and group counseling to address emotional distress, anxiety, depression, grief, and adjustment to illness

· Deliver crisis intervention and short-term supportive counseling as needed

· Conduct comprehensive psychosocial assessments and develop individualized care plans

· Use evidence-based therapeutic modalities (e.g., CBT, ACT, supportive psychotherapy) tailored to oncology patients

· Support decision-making around treatment, survivorship, and end-of-life care

· Collaborate with oncology providers, nurses, navigators, and behavioral health team members to ensure whole-person care

· Participate in multidisciplinary case reviews, tumor boards, and team rounds

· Educate patients and caregivers on coping strategies, stress management, and mental wellness

· Facilitate or support group interventions, workshops, or wellness initiatives for patients and families

· Document all patient interactions and interventions in the EMR clearly, accurately, and in a timely manner

· Ensure compliance with all ethical, licensure, and regulatory standards

· Engage in continuing education and participate in quality improvement initiatives

· Support care navigation requests for patients



Reporting: This role will report to the Practice Manager



Required Qualifications

· Master’s degree in Social Work (MSW) from an accredited institution

· Active LICSW license in the state of Tennessee

· Minimum of 2 years of counseling experience in a healthcare setting

· Proficiency in therapeutic modalities applicable to psychosocial oncology care

· Excellent communication, documentation, and interpersonal skills

· Ability to navigate sensitive clinical situations with compassion and professionalism

· Comfort with change, process improvement, and working in a start-up or growing organization



Preferred Qualifications

· Certification in Oncology Social Work (OSW-C) or similar specialization

· Experience working with oncology, palliative care, or chronic illness populations

· Experience leading or facilitating support groups or workshops

· Bilingual or multilingual skills preferred but not required

· Experience working with diverse populations and an understanding of health disparities

· Experience with athenahealth





Some of the benefits of our comprehensive benefits package includes:

· Health, Dental, Vision insurance

· Competitive pay

· Paid vacation, holidays, and sick leave



You will collaborate with like-minded

source_jobs.title: Care Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Role: Oncology Care Coordinator

Company: Atlas Oncology Partners

Location: On-site; Rotating in-person clinic at Mobile and Baldwin County

Position Type: Full-Time



About Us

Atlas is built for oncologists by oncologists. We provide practices with the staff, contracts, and toolkit they need to deliver truly whole person care to their cancer patients. Our value-based approach to care delivery aligns incentives, offering patients better outcomes, payors guaranteed reduction in medical expense, and provider partners greater financial independence.



Role Description

At Atlas Oncology Partners, we believe every patient deserves personalized support as they navigate the complex cancer care system. We are seeking a dedicated Oncology Care Coordinator to serve as a consistent, non-clinical point of contact for patients and families throughout the cancer journey. This role is designed to reduce barriers to care, improve access to timely services, and provide practical and emotional support through close coordination with patients, providers, and interdisciplinary team members. The Oncology Care Coordinator ensures no patient faces cancer alone. As a small nimble team, this role will support vaccinations and intermittent phlebotomy in addition to care coordination.



Responsibilities include, but not limited to, the following:

· Serve as a primary point of contact for patients and families, helping them navigate appointments, treatment, and care coordination

· Help connect patients to accurate, up-to-date educational resources regarding their diagnosis and treatment, as directed by the clinical care team.

· Screens for potential barriers to care and collaborates with appropriate team members to address logistical, financial, cultural, and psychosocial needs

· Assist with scheduling and follow-up appointments across the oncology continuum (e.g., surgery, infusion, radiation)

· Support connection to support groups, counseling, social work, and other internal or community-based services

· Establish trusting relationships with patients and caregivers through regular outreach and touchpoints

· Participate in interdisciplinary meetings and escalate patient concerns or needs appropriately

· Ensure patients complete key milestones, such as diagnostics, consults, and treatment starts

· Document patient interactions and support activities in the EMR clearly and in a timely manner

· Work closely with RN Care Coordinators, Social Workers, and Clinical Teams to ensure seamless, wraparound care

· Support in phlebotomy and vaccinations, as needed



Reporting: This role will report to the Practice Manager



Required Qualifications

· Bachelor’s degree

· Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including empathy and cultural sensitivity

· Proficiency in EMR use, phone communication, and task tracking systems

· Comfort with change, process improvement, and working in a start-up or growing organization

· Experience in phlebotomy or Medical Assistant License



Preferred Qualifications

· Bachelor’s degree in social work, Public Health, Nursing, Health Education, or a related field

· Oncology Patient Navigator Certification

· Experience in patient navigation, care coordination, oncology, or a healthcare support role

· Experience working with diverse populations and addressing social determinants of health

· Detail-oriented and highly organized; able to manage multiple patient caseloads and prioritize tasks

· Experience advocating for patients and helping them access financial, transportation, or housing assistance

· Comfortable speaking with patients by phone or in person to provide guidance, reminders, and support

· Team player who thrives in fast-paced, mission-driven healthcare environments



Some of the benefits of our comprehensive benefits package includes:

· Health, Dental, Vision insurance

· Competitive pay

· Paid vacation, holidays, and sick leave



You will collaborate with like-minded health care profe

source_jobs.title: Clinic Supervisor
source_jobs.description:
Role: Office Supervisor

Company: Atlas Oncology Partners

Location: On-site; Memphis

Position Type: Full-Time



About Us

Atlas is built for oncologists by oncologists. We provide practices with the staff, contracts, and tools they need to deliver whole-person care to their cancer patients. Our value-based approach to care delivery aligns incentives, offering patients better outcomes, payors guaranteed reduction in medical expense, and provider partners greater financial independence.



Role Description

At Atlas Oncology Partners, we believe an exceptional patient experience begins the moment a patient walks through our doors. We are seeking a dedicated and organized Office Supervisor to serve as the operational backbone of our clinic, keeping daily workflows running smoothly, supporting our care team, and ensuring every patient interaction reflects the compassionate, high-quality care we are committed to providing.

This role is the connective tissue between our front-of-house operations, Oncology partner, our clinical team, and the person in it sets the tone for how our office functions day to day. Coordination of our stakeholders and team is critical to success.

As part of a small, nimble team, this role requires someone who is equally comfortable jumping in wherever needed, owning processes from start to finish, and supporting the people around them. The Office Supervisor reports to the Operations Director and serves as the on-site lead for day-to-day office operations, with direct management responsibility for non-provider care team members.



Responsibilities include, but not limited to, the following:



Responsibilities

· Serve as the direct manager for non-provider care team members.

· Serve as the on-site lead and subject matter expert on office workflows and processes, and champion best practices with patients and team members.

· Manage daily and same-day scheduling, including booking, confirming, and rescheduling appointments, infusion visits, and follow-ups; publish monthly shift schedules for administrative staff under the direction of the Operations Director.

· Ensure operational readiness through regular team check-ins, facilitating daily office huddles, monitoring task queues, and reviewing staffing and adherence to standard work.

· Perform inventory management: order and receive office and non-clinical supplies, track expirations, minimize waste, and complete regular counts within the supply budget.

· Handle all front-of-house duties: check-in/check-out, insurance verification, copay collection, paperwork and records requests, phones, and follow-up scheduling.

· Support the financial health of the practice through copay collection, accurate demographic and insurance capture, and routing updates to the billing team.

· Monitor patient feedback and respond to service-recovery issues, escalating to the Operations Director needed.

· Support new team members through training, onboarding logistics, mentoring, and team recognition.

· Maintain the appearance, organization, and readiness of patient- and employee-facing spaces, including opening and closing duties, daily upkeep, restocking, and organization.

· Coordinate facilities, security, IT, and vendor requests as encountered.



Qualifications

· At least 1 year of experience in a supervisory or lead role in high-touch customer service or patient-facing healthcare.

· Proficient in customer service, administrative work, and motivating team members and peers, especially through times of change.

· Skilled in nuanced, compassionate patient communication with an emphasis on consistent, high-quality patient-centered experiences, essential in oncology care.

· Proven ability to foster strong, collaborative team dynamics and a supportive, engaged team culture.

· Experience working on collaborative, diverse, feedback-driven multi-disciplinary teams.

· Strong written and verbal communication skills.

· Comfortable with scheduling software, multi-line p
